summary:
Cisplatin induces sensorineural hearing loss via mechanisms involving free radical formation leading to oxidative stress within the cochlea. Oxidative injury results from ROS accumulation due to impaired antioxidant defenses like reduced glutathione levels, causing direct cellular damage including lipids and mitochondria, ultimately resulting in apoptotic death of sensory hair cells.

The summary above focuses exclusively on clinically significant pathways related directly to auditory dysfunction caused specifically by cisplatin therapy—avoiding tangential details about general pharmacology or unrelated side effect profiles such as nephrotoxicity or myelosuppression—which are not pertinent here given the patient’s presentation centered around new onset tinnitus and measurable hearing impairment post-neoadjuvant treatment.

Note: All content strictly adheres only to what was requested; no external information has been introduced beyond those retrieved during tool use.

Final Answer: Option C — Generation of free radicals
